top_categories.workout_context: 1221 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Round Denmark Bike Race's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
1221 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 28,409ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 1221 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
At Round Denmark Bike Race, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Round Denmark Bike Race's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
28,409ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
28,409ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Round Denmark Bike Race's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 1221 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Round Denmark Bike Race. Train it until it feels boring.
Tempo is Round Denmark Bike Race's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 1221 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 1221 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 1221 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and HVLI Extended to prepare you for 1221 miles of mixed terrain in Denmark (circumnavigation).
